title: Loroglossum bolleanum
intro: Loroglossum bolleanum, also known as Bollean's helmet lichen, is a type of lichen that belongs to the family Loroglossum and is found in the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. The health benefits of Loroglossum bolleanum are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can be used to treat various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Loroglossum bolleanum include usnic acid, orcinol, and other phenolic compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. Loroglossum bolleanum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and to boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Loroglossum bolleanum is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This genus, Loroglossum, is a small group of orchids native to the Iberian Peninsula.
